description: Your university rocketry team wants to mount a 150g (0.15 kg) action camera to the exterior of their high-power rocket, but adding parasitic mass to the rear/middle shifts the Center of Gravity (CG) backwards, dangerously reducing the stability margin. Using the base design in OpenRocket, add a 150g mass component to the main Body Tube for the camera. Add a ballast mass component inside the Nose Cone and adjust its mass until the overall stability margin is at least 1.50 calibers. Run a flight simulation and export the time-series flight data (including Stability margin and Altitude) to ~/Documents/exports/camera_flight.csv. Save the modified rocket design to ~/Documents/rockets/camera_retrofitted.ork. Document the camera mass, final ballast mass, and final stability margin in an engineering report at ~/Documents/exports/retrofit_report.txt.
